subject: Project Status Update | Week of April 10, 2026 — Mixed Progress, Key Escalations Underway
introduction: Dear Stakeholders, this week's update reflects a mixed but actively managed portfolio. We are pleased to report the successful completion of the Global Logistics Migration, while navigating a vendor-driven delay to Project Titan and a time-sensitive escalation on the SSO Expansion. Engineering leadership has taken deliberate steps to protect delivery timelines and revenue-critical commitments. Below is a full summary of current project statuses, risks, and actions in flight.

sections.heading: ✅ Completed — Global Logistics Migration
items: Status: 100% Complete | Originally Due: March 20, 2026

The manual migration has been successfully completed. The client has been stabilized and has formally accepted the previously communicated delay following a review of the Root Cause Analysis (RCA) documentation.

Important Note: This account remains high-touch. The client is actively monitoring response times on all future support tickets and has communicated zero tolerance for further delays. Account teams should treat all incoming tickets from this client as priority-sensitive until further notice.

sections.heading: ⚠️ Delayed — Project Titan (65% Complete | Revised Due Date: April 30, 2026)
items: Status: High Priority | Delayed | Previously Due: April 15, 2026

Project Titan's launch date has been revised from April 15 to April 30, 2026. The delay is primarily attributable to a breaking API change introduced without a sunset period by our third-party payment gateway vendor, which deprecated the existing library powering the Titan checkout flow. A complete rewrite of the checkout integration module is now required, adding approximately 40 additional engineering hours to the schedule.
Compounding the delay: 12 Priority 2 (P2) bugs were surfaced during Beta testing and must be resolved prior to launch.

Additional resource impact: One engineer has been temporarily reallocated from Titan to the SSO Expansion (see below) to protect a $200K enterprise revenue opportunity. Engineering is actively managing capacity to mitigate further slippage.
Current completion: 65%. The team remains focused on the April 30 delivery target.

sections.heading: 🚨 Escalated to Active Priority — SSO Expansion (30% Complete | Target: End of This Week)
items: Status: High Priority | In Progress | Previously Due: March 27, 2026

SSO Expansion has been elevated from a 21-day backlog stall to active development status, effective immediately. This escalation is business-critical: delivery of SSO Expansion is a hard prerequisite for closing a $200K contract with OmniCorp.
One engineer has been reallocated from Project Titan to accelerate completion. The revised target delivery date is end of this week.
Stakeholders should be aware that this reallocation creates a temporary resource constraint on Titan. Both timelines are being actively monitored.

sections.heading: 🔧 In Progress — Third-Party Payment Gateway Integration (15% Complete | Due: April 30, 2026)
items: Status: High Priority | Delayed | Due: April 30, 2026

Engineering has begun the full rewrite of the payment gateway integration module following the vendor's unannounced breaking API change. This work is tightly coupled to Project Titan's revised April 30 launch date.

A formal vendor escalation has been assigned to David (Solutions Consultant) to challenge the vendor's change management practices and document the impact of the unsupported deprecation. This escalation is intended to establish accountability and reduce the likelihood of similar disruptions in future vendor relationships.

sections.heading: 🔄 In Progress — On-Call Rotation & Bug Lead Program (10% Complete | Due: March 20, 2026)
items: Status: Medium Priority | In Progress

In response to a 4-hour missed SLA incident on March 19, Engineering is establishing a daily rotating 'Bug Lead' role designed to absorb P0 support escalations and prevent senior engineers from being pulled off critical project deliverables mid-sprint.

Action due today: Jamie (Engineering Lead) is responsible for publishing the initial on-call schedule to Slack by end of day. Stakeholders can expect to see the rotation formalized and visible to the broader engineering organization by close of business.

sections.heading: 📊 Portfolio Metrics at a Glance
items: Total tracked items: 5 projects + 1 operational program
High Priority: 3 | In Progress: 2 | Completed: 1 | On Schedule: 0
Overall portfolio status: Mixed — one delivery milestone achieved; two projects impacted by external vendor dependency and resource reallocation; one revenue-critical item now in active remediation.

conclusion: Despite a challenging week driven largely by an external vendor's unmanaged API change, the team has responded decisively — escalating the SSO Expansion to protect a key revenue commitment, initiating a formal vendor accountability process, and putting structural safeguards in place to protect engineering velocity going forward. We will continue to provide updates as Project Titan and SSO Expansion approach their respective delivery targets. Please reach out directly with any questions or concerns ahead of the next scheduled review.
